name: Rice Paddy Dike Section
prompt:
Vietnamese rice paddy earthen dike/berm section. Low earthen wall between flooded rice paddies, used as cover by soldiers.

Physical structure:
- Dike: elongated earthen mound, ~3m long, ~0.5m tall, ~0.8m wide at base
  - Trapezoidal cross-section (wider at base, narrower at top)
  - Slightly irregular surface (not perfectly smooth)
  - Flat-ish top (~0.3m wide)
  - Gentle slopes on both sides
- Top: some grass/vegetation on top
  - Small flat green patches
- Sides: bare earth, muddy
  - Some water marks/staining at base
- Base: sits in shallow water (the paddy water level)

Colors: Muddy brown earth (0x6B5530), darker wet earth at base (0x4a3a20), green vegetation patches on top (0x5a7a3a), water stain (0x4a4a30)
Orientation: Dike runs along X axis, height along Y
Scale: Real-world meters (~3m long, ~0.5m tall, ~0.8m wide base)
